messages.content: Where the idea of Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go! came for? It’s was going to be pitched on cartoon network before move to disney? in 1999, Ciro Nieli launch his studio called Nebulous Films which he want to make his shows he want to be.

One of those shows was Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go! Nieli was inspired by Japanese anime and the idea of a team of heroes piloting giant robots to battle evil forces. He developed the concept and characters, and the show was eventually pitched to Cartoon Network. However, the network passed on it, and it was eventually picked up by Disney, where it aired from 2004 to 2006.
was Nebulous Films still closed down?

It's unclear what the current status of Nebulous Films is. The studio was founded by Ciro Nieli in 1999 and produced several animated TV shows, including Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go! and Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles. However, it appears that the studio has been inactive for some time, with no new productions announced in recent years. It's possible that the studio has closed down, but there is no official confirmation of this.
was nebulous make web shorts and try to pitch series in early 2000s before it was close down?

It's possible that Nebulous Films made web shorts and tried to pitch series in the early 2000s before its closure, but there is no information online that confirms this. It's worth noting that Nebulous Films was active during a time when the internet was still developing as a platform for animation, so it's possible that the studio may have tested the waters with web shorts or other online content. However, it's unclear if any of this content was made public or had any impact on the studio's operations.
since of Super Robot Monkey Team was a show in japan, it's was originally going to be in flash? and also the the original title name was Ultra Force Go! before the change?

As far as I could research, Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go! was not originally a show in Japan, but was created by Ciro Nieli and his team at Nebulous Films. Additionally, I could not find any information suggesting that the show was going to be animated in Flash. It's possible that some parts of the animation process may have involved Flash, which was a popular tool for web animation at the time, but it's unlikely that the entire show was animated in Flash. 

As for the original title, there is some information that suggests that the show was originally going to be called "Ultra Force Go!" before it was changed to "Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go!" However, I could not find any definitive sources that confirm this. It's possible that the original title was used during the show's development or early promotion but was ultimately changed for various reasons.
Was The city of Shuggazoom was instead called Pachinko Town?

I could not find any information that suggests that the City of Shuggazoom from Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go! was originally called Pachinko Town. It's possible that the name of the city may have been changed during the show's development, but I could not find any definitive sources that confirm this. Additionally, "Pachinko Town" does not appear to be a commonly used name for any city or location in the show, so it seems unlikely that this was the original name for the show's main setting.
what if, Ultra Force Go! still be keep the numerous major differences and be made, where does effect the show?

If Ultra Force Go! had been made instead of Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go! with the same major differences, it's possible that the show would have been received differently by audiences and critics. 

For one, the title "Ultra Force Go!" doesn't have the same ring as "Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go!" and may not have been as catchy or memorable. This could have potentially affected the show's ability to build a strong fanbase or gain widespread recognition.

Additionally, the show's premise and characters may have been viewed as less original or interesting than Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go!. The "Ultra Force" name could have been seen as derivative of other super-powered hero teams like the Power Rangers or the Avengers, and the show's animal-based heroes may not have had the same appeal as the robotic monkeys from Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go!.

Overall, it's difficult to say how Ultra Force Go! would have been received if it had been made instead of Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go!, as it's impossible to predict how audiences and markets would have responded to the show.

why did Antauri was the leader of the Monkey Team, and Chiro was instead a younger Tagalong Kid in the  Early pitch materials? why does Roles and personality traits being swapped around. For example, early Nova was said to "try her best to be sweet, gentile, and peaceful", whereas the final Nova more boisterous? and why The Super Robot had no weaponry of its own, and resorted to fisticuffs rather than the final version's missiles and lasers?

It's not uncommon for major changes to occur during the development of animated shows, and one reason for this is the evolution of the creative process. As ideas are fleshed out and characters take shape, creators may find that certain roles and personality traits don't work as well as they originally intended. 

In the case of Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go!, it's possible that the initial pitch materials had Chiro as a younger Tagalong Kid and Antauri as the leader because the creators were experimenting with different character dynamics. As the development process continued, however, they may have found that the Monkey Team flowed better with Antauri as the leader and Chiro in a more supportive role.

Similarly, the evolution of the characters' personalities could have resulted from trial and error during the concept development process. The creators may have tried different personality traits for the same character before settling on the ones that worked best for the show.

Finally, the changes to the Super Robot's weaponry could have been reflective of the creators' desire to make the show more exciting and action-packed. Fisticuffs may have been viewed as less engaging for viewers than missiles and lasers, so the weapons were added to make the fight scenes more visually exciting.
if Skeleton King was not going to be voice by Mark Hamill?

It's difficult to say how much of an impact changing the voice actor for Skeleton King would have had on Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go!. 

Mark Hamill's voice performance is widely regarded as one of the standout features of the show, and his work as the Joker in Batman: The Animated Series helped establish him as a talented voice actor. That being said, there are many talented voice actors who could have been brought on to voice the character if Hamill had not been available. 

If a different actor had voiced Skeleton King, it's possible that the character would have had a different personality or tone, depending on the actor's interpretation. However, it's difficult to say how this would have affected the overall tone of the show, as the dynamic between the Monkey Team and Skeleton King is the result of many factors beyond just the voice performance. Ultimately, whether or not the character was voiced by Mark Hamill, Skeleton King would still have been an integral part of the show's story and conflict.
was Chiro not going to voiced by Greg Cipes but a kid will voice him?

It's difficult to confirm whether or not Chiro was initially going to be voiced by a child actor rather than Greg Cipes. However, given that Chiro is one of the main characters in Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go!, it's unlikely that he would have been voiced throughout the series by a child actor alone. 

Using child actors to voice child characters is a common practice in animation, but it's important to remember that actors with more experience and training are often better suited to bringing characters to life. Additionally, the creators of Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go! may have wanted a more consistent vocal performance for the character, which can be difficult to achieve with a child actor as their voices can change over time. 

That being said, it's possible that a child actor may have been used to voice the character in a few specific instances, such as flashback scenes or moments when Chiro is shown as a younger child. But given the complexity of Chiro's character and his role in the show, it's likely that a more experienced actor like Greg Cipes was seen as essential to bringing the character to life.

was Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go! in Production in mid 2003 before jetix launch in 2004? which premiered on ABC Family's Jetix programming block on September 18, 2004.

Yes, Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go! was in production in mid-2003 before Jetix launched in 2004. The show first began development around 2002-2003, and the initial 13 episodes were produced from July 2003 to March 2004. The show was then picked up by ABC Family's Jetix programming block and premiered in September of 2004. 

In the years leading up to Super Robot Monkey Team Hyperforce Go!, Jetix was known as Fox Kids worldwide. After being acquired by the Walt Disney Company, Fox Kids was renamed Jetix in 2004. Jetix was then later merged with Toon Disney to become Disney XD in 2009.
